title: quercus castaneifolia var. typica
intro: Quercus castaneifolia var. typica, also known as the golden oak, is a variety of the Quercus castaneifolia tree species, native to the mountainous regions of China. This tree has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus castaneifolia var. typica include reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and treating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The herbal preparations of Quercus castaneifolia var. typica include dried leaves and bark, which can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
